name: Avalon Credit Repair Company
description_short: Avalon Credit Repair offers premium credit repair and debt settlement counseling with 14 years of experience, helping clients dispute inaccuracies and rebuild credit scores.
description_long:
Avalon Credit Repair Company is a credit repair service claiming 14 years of experience in the credit restoration industry. The company positions itself as a premium counseling service focused on helping clients identify and address negative items on their credit reports.

Avalon offers a range of credit repair services including dispute resolution for inaccurate items, charge-off removal, identity theft services, debt settlement negotiation, and specialized services for judgments, liens, repossessions, foreclosures, and student loan issues. They claim to work directly with credit bureaus and creditors to challenge and remove unfair or inaccurate entries. The company structures services around different client scenarios—those without public records, those with charge-offs, and those with multiple debt obligations.

The company emphasizes personalized consultation and customized strategies, starting with a credit report review to identify negative items before creating a tailored action plan. They market themselves as offering "affordable plans" and positioning expert advisors as a key differentiator. Their process includes direct negotiation with creditors for debt settlement and claims of efficient credit restoration.

A critical caveat exists: the website contains a significant inconsistency, referring to "Galleria Credit Repair Company" in the process section while branding as "Avalon Credit Repair." This raises questions about company identity and operational clarity. Additionally, like all credit repair services, their ability to remove accurate negative information is legally limited—they can only dispute inaccuracies or items lacking proper verification.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 14 years claimed industry experience in credit repair and debt counseling
Offers specialized services for specific situations: charge-offs, identity theft, judgments, liens, repossessions, foreclosures, and student loans
Claims personalized consultation process starting with thorough credit report review
Negotiates debt settlements directly with creditors to reduce owed amounts
Provides tiered service options (Premium & VIP) designed for different client profiles
Markets affordable pricing plans for credit repair services
cons: Website contains branding inconsistency, referencing 'Galleria Credit Repair Company' in process section—raises identity and operational clarity concerns
No transparent pricing information displayed; claims 'affordable plans' without specifics
No verifiable credentials, certifications, or licensing information provided on website
Cannot legally remove accurate negative information from credit reports, only dispute inaccuracies
No client testimonials, case studies, or performance metrics disclosed to demonstrate results
best_for: Consumers with charged-off accounts and collection accounts seeking dispute resolution
Individuals with multiple negative items (judgments, liens, repossessions) wanting coordinated removal strategy
People with identity theft impacts on credit reports needing specialized remediation
Borrowers with outstanding debts open to settlement negotiations to reduce balances
diagnosis: Avalon Credit Repair is appropriate for consumers with multiple negative credit items (charge-offs, collections, judgments, liens) who want professional assistance disputing inaccuracies and negotiating debt settlements. The main caveat: verify company credentials and clarify the Avalon/Galleria branding inconsistency before engaging, and understand that credit repair services can only dispute inaccurate or unverifiable items—not legitimate negative information.
services: Student loan consolidation and reporting correction
Premium and VIP service tier options
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
